wines.name: Gaja Barbaresco

taste.summary: Gaja's village Barbaresco blends fruit from several Barbaresco crus into a rich, round Nebbiolo with tarry depth, grainy tannin and a voluminous finish.

pairings.why: Slow-braised beef in Nebbiolo echoes the wine's tar and dried-cherry register and softens against its firm tannin.

pairings.dish: Tajarin with butter and white truffle
pairings.why: The wine's savoury earth lifts the truffle while its acidity cuts the egg-rich pasta.
Alpine cow's-milk salt sharpens Nebbiolo's red-fruit lift.

history.summary: The Gaja family has bottled Barbaresco since 1859. Angelo Gaja modernised the village blend from the 1960s, championing Nebbiolo from Barbaresco on the world stage.

milestones.event: Angelo Gaja joins the family estate and begins modernising
Single-vineyard crus returned to the Barbaresco DOCG label after the 1996 to 2012 Langhe Nebbiolo declassification

wines.description: Gaja's village Barbaresco, a multi-cru Nebbiolo blend that put the appellation on the global map. Rich, tarry and built for the cellar.

wines.name: Gaja Sori Tildin

taste.summary: A south-facing cru above Costa Russi, Sori Tildin is the smoky, elegantly austere face of Gaja's Barbaresco: graphite, licorice and firm yet polished tannin over a refreshing spine of acidity.

pairings.why: Long-braised collagen melts against firm tannin while the dish's depth meets the wine's licorice register.
Earthy truffle resonates with the cru's graphite and savoury core.
Crystalline salt sharpens the red-currant lift.

history.summary: Sori Tildin debuted with the 1970 vintage from a 3.1-hectare south-facing parcel above Costa Russi. The name joins sori, a south-facing hillside in dialect, with Tildin, the diminutive of Clotilde, Angelo Gaja's grandmother.

milestones.event: First single-vineyard Sori Tildin vintage
Returned to Barbaresco DOCG label

wines.description: Gaja's most celebrated Barbaresco cru, a south-facing hill above Costa Russi. Smoky, austere and built for decades in the cellar.

wines.name: Gaja Sori San Lorenzo

taste.summary: The most austere and powerful of Gaja's Barbaresco crus, Sori San Lorenzo shows graphite, licorice and dried cherry over firm youthful tannin and a refreshing acid spine.

pairings.why: Gamey richness meets the cru's austere structure and savoury depth.
Mushroom earth resonates with the wine's graphite and forest-floor notes.
Salt and umami sharpen the red-currant lift.

history.summary: Sori San Lorenzo, from a parcel below the church of San Lorenzo in Alba, was Gaja's first single-vineyard Barbaresco, debuting with the 1967 vintage.

milestones.event: First single-vineyard Sori San Lorenzo vintage
Returned to Barbaresco DOCG label

wines.description: Gaja's first single-vineyard Barbaresco, from a parcel below Alba's San Lorenzo church. The most austere and structured of the trio.

wines.name: Gaja Costa Russi

taste.summary: The most charming and aromatic of Gaja's Barbaresco crus, Costa Russi pairs an abundance of strawberry and red fruit with verbena and marzipan, silky tannin and an elegant finish.

pairings.why: Delicate game meets the cru's perfumed red fruit without overwhelming it.
The wine's elegant red-fruit lift carries the meat-stuffed pasta's richness.
Mild alpine cheese frames the wine's strawberry and elderflower.

history.summary: Costa Russi debuted with the 1978 vintage from a four-hectare parcel just below Sori Tildin. The name joins costa, meaning slope, with Russi, the nickname of the vineyard's former sharecropper.

milestones.event: First single-vineyard Costa Russi vintage
Returned to Barbaresco DOCG label

wines.description: The most perfumed and charming of Gaja's three Barbaresco crus, just below Sori Tildin. Silky and red-fruited with marzipan lift.

wines.name: Gaja Sperss

taste.summary: Gaja's Serralunga Barolo from the Marenca-Rivette parcels: deep, mineral and austere with sour black cherry, truffle and a decidedly salty, stony finish.

pairings.why: The Piedmontese mixed boiled meats meet the wine's structure and salty finish.
Rich collagen melts against the firm Serralunga tannin.
Hard alpine cheese mirrors the wine's mineral spine.

history.summary: Sperss, dialect for nostalgia, marked Gaja's return to Barolo in 1988 with the purchase of the Marenca-Rivette vineyard in Serralunga d'Alba, land the family had not held for decades.

milestones.event: First Sperss vintage from Serralunga d'Alba
Returned to Barolo DOCG label

wines.description: Gaja's Serralunga Barolo from the Marenca-Rivette site, marking the family's return to Barolo in 1988. Deep, salty and structured.

wines.name: Gaja Conteisa

taste.summary: From the Cerequio amphitheatre in La Morra, Conteisa is the flowery, fresh and aromatic side of Gaja's Barolo, with sweet wild cherry, herbs and fine-grained chalky tannin.

pairings.why: The wine's fresh red fruit and acidity lift the cold veal and tuna sauce.
Delicate game meets Conteisa's perfumed, herb-edged fruit.
Mild alpine cheese frames the chalky tannin and cherry lift.

history.summary: Conteisa comes from Gaja's holdings in the Cerequio cru on the La Morra side of Barolo. Its name recalls a centuries-long border dispute between La Morra and Barolo over the prized hill.

milestones.event: First Conteisa vintage from Cerequio in La Morra
Returned to Barolo DOCG label

wines.description: Gaja's Barolo from the Cerequio cru in La Morra, named for an old boundary dispute. Flowery, fresh and chalky-tannined.

wines.name: Vietti Barolo Rocche di Castiglione

taste.summary: From the steep Rocche di Castiglione cru, Vietti's most aromatic Barolo shows perfumed red cherry and rose with fine-grained tannin and a long, mineral finish.

pairings.why: The braise's depth meets the cru's red-fruit perfume and firm tannin.
Truffle earth resonates with the wine's mineral, savoury core.
Salty alpine cheese sharpens the red-cherry lift.

history.summary: Vietti has bottled single-vineyard Barolo from the Rocche di Castiglione cru in Castiglione Falletto for decades, one of the zone's most perfumed and elegant sites.

milestones.event: Vietti releases early single-cru Barolo bottlings under Alfredo Currado

wines.description: Vietti's perfumed Barolo from the steep Rocche di Castiglione cru. Rose and red cherry over fine-grained tannin.

wines.name: Vietti Barolo Lazzarito

taste.summary: From the Serralunga cru of Lazzarito, this is Vietti's most robust, potent Barolo: hay, tobacco and white pepper over stark, profound tannin and a taut mineral finish.

pairings.why: Potent tannin needs the richness of long-braised beef.
Serralunga structure stands up to the platter of mixed boiled meats.
Hard alpine cheese mirrors the wine's mineral grip.

history.summary: Lazzarito is one of Serralunga d'Alba's grand crus, giving Vietti a powerful, structured Barolo from the zone's iron-rich soils.

milestones.event: Vietti bottles Barolo from the Lazzarito cru in Serralunga

wines.description: Vietti's powerful Serralunga Barolo from the Lazzarito cru. Hay, tobacco and white pepper over stark, profound tannin.

wines.name: Vietti Barolo Ravera

taste.summary: From the Ravera cru in Novello, this is a very elegant, refined Vietti Barolo: complex floral and chalk-edged red fruit with taut, compact tannin and a vibrant, vertical acid line.

pairings.why: The wine's vertical acidity cuts the rich meat-stuffed pasta.
Refined tannin frames the delicate meat without overpowering it.
Semi-firm alpine cheese meets the chalky red fruit.

history.summary: Vietti added the Ravera cru in Novello to its Barolo range in the late 2000s, prizing its elegant, chalk-driven expression of Nebbiolo.

milestones.event: Vietti first bottles Barolo from the Ravera cru

wines.description: Vietti's elegant, chalk-driven Barolo from the Ravera cru in Novello. Floral red fruit over taut, vertical tannin.

wines.name: Vietti Barolo Brunate

taste.summary: From the famed Brunate cru in La Morra, Vietti's Barolo is explosive and powerful, with plum and citrusy cedar, the violet typical of the site and soft, velvety La Morra tannin.

pairings.why: Rich braised beef matches the wine's powerful fruit and velvety tannin.
Mushroom earth meets the wine's liquorice-root depth.
Salty cheese sharpens the violet and plum lift.

history.summary: Brunate in La Morra is one of Barolo's most celebrated crus, giving Vietti a powerful yet velvety expression of Nebbiolo.

milestones.event: Vietti's early single-cru Barolo programme includes Brunate

wines.description: Vietti's powerful yet velvety Barolo from the Brunate cru in La Morra. Plum, cedar and the cru's signature violet.

wines.name: Vietti Barolo Cerequio

taste.summary: From the Cerequio cru on the La Morra and Barolo boundary, this is an elegant, imposing Vietti Barolo with strawberry, plum and cherry, enveloping body and a persistent balsamic finish.

pairings.why: Imposing tannin meets the richness of slow-braised beef.
The wine's red fruit carries the egg-rich pasta.
Alpine cheese frames the balsamic finish.

history.summary: Cerequio is a prized cru straddling La Morra and Barolo, giving Vietti a harmonious, complex and age-worthy Barolo.

milestones.event: Vietti bottles Barolo from the Cerequio cru

wines.description: Vietti's harmonious Barolo from the Cerequio cru on the La Morra and Barolo boundary. Strawberry, plum and a balsamic finish.

wines.name: Vietti Barbera d'Asti La Crena

taste.summary: From old vines planted in 1932, La Crena is a serious, age-worthy Barbera with ruby depth, ripe blackberry and cherry and spicy notes over Barbera's signature racy acidity.

pairings.why: Barbera's bright acidity cuts the rich meat filling.
The wine's fruit and acidity refresh the palate against cured fats.
A regional match where the wine echoes the dish's own base.

history.summary: La Crena comes from a Barbera vineyard in Agliano Terme whose oldest section dates to 1932, giving one of the region's most structured, age-worthy Barberas.

milestones.event: Oldest section of the La Crena vineyard planted
Vietti bottles La Crena as a single-vineyard Barbera

wines.description: Vietti's old-vine Barbera d'Asti from 1932-planted La Crena. Structured, age-worthy and built on racy acidity.

wines.name: Vietti Barbera d'Alba Vigna Scarrone

taste.summary: From the Scarrone vineyard in Castiglione Falletto, a ruby, full-bodied Barbera with ripe red and black cherry, vanilla and spice, good acidity and a complex, persistent finish.

pairings.why: Barbera's acidity lifts the cold veal and tuna sauce.
The wine's fruit and acidity carry the meat sauce.
Cured pork meets the wine's refreshing acid line.

history.summary: Scarrone is a Barbera vineyard within the Castiglione Falletto Barolo zone, where Vietti has long made a serious, oak-aged Barbera d'Alba.

milestones.event: Vietti bottles Barbera d'Alba from the Scarrone vineyard

wines.description: Vietti's oak-aged Barbera d'Alba from the Scarrone vineyard in Castiglione Falletto. Ripe cherry, vanilla and bright acidity.

wines.name: Vietti Langhe Nebbiolo Perbacco

taste.summary: Vietti's Langhe Nebbiolo from declassified Barolo fruit: raspberry, currant, mint and spice with round, elegant tannins and a refined finish that drinks well young.

pairings.why: The wine's round tannin and red fruit carry the meat sauce.
Medium body suits poultry without overwhelming it.
Mild cheese frames the raspberry and mint.

history.summary: Perbacco is Vietti's Langhe Nebbiolo, drawn largely from young vines in Barolo crus, made as an approachable, earlier-drinking introduction to the grape.

milestones.event: Perbacco launched as Vietti's Langhe Nebbiolo

wines.description: Vietti's approachable Langhe Nebbiolo from declassified Barolo fruit. Raspberry, mint and round, elegant tannin.

wines.name: Vietti Roero Arneis

taste.summary: A straw-yellow, dry, medium-bodied Arneis with fresh floral, citrus and melon aromas, mineral and almond notes, fresh acidity and notable persistence.

pairings.why: Arneis's citrus and almond lift the cold veal and tuna.
Fresh acidity cuts the fried fish and vegetables.
Mineral, floral Arneis frames freshwater fish.

history.summary: Vietti was among the producers who helped revive Arneis, the white grape of the Roero, from near-extinction in the late 1960s.

milestones.event: Vietti bottles one of the first modern Roero Arneis wines

wines.description: Vietti's Roero Arneis, from a grape the house helped revive. Floral, citrus and almond with fresh acidity.

wines.name: Vietti Moscato d'Asti Cascinetta

taste.summary: A bright straw-gold, slightly sparkling Moscato d'Asti with intense peach, apricot, rose petal and ginger, sweet and delicate with modest acidity and good balance.

pairings.why: Piedmont's classic nut cake meets the wine's peachy sweetness.
The wine's delicate sweetness and rose lift the creamy custard.
An echo pairing of stone fruit and sweetness.

history.summary: Cascinetta is Vietti's Moscato d'Asti, a lightly sparkling, low-alcohol sweet wine from the Moscato Bianco grape of the Asti hills.

milestones.event: Vietti releases the Cascinetta Moscato d'Asti

wines.description: Vietti's lightly sparkling, low-alcohol Moscato d'Asti. Peach, apricot and rose petal with delicate sweetness.

wines.name: Vietti Dolcetto d'Alba Tre Vigne

taste.summary: A purplish-red Dolcetto with subtle violet hues, blackberry, raspberry and violet aromas and mineral, blueberry notes, medium-bodied with soft tannins and good balance.

pairings.why: Dolcetto's juicy dark fruit and low tannin suit tomato and cheese.
The wine's soft, fruity body refreshes against cured meats.
Soft cheese and potato meet the wine's juicy fruit.

history.summary: Tre Vigne is Vietti's blend of Dolcetto from three vineyards, an everyday red showcasing the grape's juicy, low-tannin charm.

milestones.event: Vietti bottles the Tre Vigne Dolcetto blend

wines.description: Vietti's everyday Dolcetto from three vineyards. Juicy blackberry and violet with soft tannin.

wines.name: Bruno Rocca Barbaresco Rabaja

taste.summary: Bruno Rocca's flagship from the Rabaja cru: deep garnet, an embracing nose of rosehip, violet and buttery notes opening to raspberry and cherry, with soft broad tannins and a long, sumptuous finish.

pairings.why: The braise meets the cru's broad tannin and red-fruit depth.
Truffle resonates with the wine's savoury, floral core.
Salt sharpens the rosehip and cherry lift.

history.summary: Bruno Rocca began bottling in 1978 and built his reputation on the Rabaja cru in Barbaresco, one of the appellation's most prestigious sites.

milestones.event: Bruno Rocca establishes Rabaja as his flagship Barbaresco

wines.description: Bruno Rocca's flagship Barbaresco from the prestigious Rabaja cru. Rosehip, violet and cherry over soft, broad tannin.

wines.name: G.D. Vajra Barolo Bricco delle Viole

taste.summary: From the high Bricco delle Viole vineyard in Vergne, the highest corner of Barolo, Vajra's flagship is a fragrant, high-toned 100% Nebbiolo built on altitude, freshness and patient ageing.

pairings.why: Braised beef matches the wine's structure and altitude-driven freshness.
High-toned Nebbiolo lifts the truffle's earth.
Salty alpine cheese sharpens the wine's red-fruit lift.

history.summary: Bricco delle Viole is the high-altitude Vergne vineyard the Vaira family regards as the heart of their Barolo, vinified as their flagship single-cru since the late 1970s.

milestones.event: G.D. Vajra establishes Bricco delle Viole as its flagship Barolo

wines.description: G.D. Vajra's flagship Barolo from the high Bricco delle Viole vineyard in Vergne. Fragrant, high-toned Nebbiolo built for the cellar.

verified.address_quoted: Via delle Viole, 25 - Fraz. Vergne 12060 Barolo (CN)

wines.name: G.D. Vajra Barolo Albe

taste.summary: Albe is Vajra's bright, sun-named Barolo drawn from three high vineyards in Vergne, a fresh, fragrant and accessible expression of classic Nebbiolo.

pairings.why: Fresh Nebbiolo and firm tannin carry the meat sauce.
The wine's red fruit suits delicate game.
Mild cheese frames the bright fruit.

history.summary: Albe, dialect-inflected for the dawns that break three times over the Vergne hills, is Vajra's classically styled Barolo from higher-altitude parcels.

milestones.event: Vajra formalises Albe as its accessible classic Barolo

wines.description: G.D. Vajra's bright, accessible Barolo from three high Vergne vineyards. Fresh, fragrant classic Nebbiolo.

verified.address_quoted: Via delle Viole, 25 - Fraz. Vergne 12060 Barolo (CN)

wines.name: G.D. Vajra Langhe Riesling Petracine

taste.summary: A rare Langhe Riesling and a pioneering Vajra project, Petracine is a dry, mineral, high-acid white from sandstone soils in the high hills of Barolo.

pairings.why: Mineral Riesling frames freshwater fish.
High acidity lifts the cold veal and tuna.
Racy acid cuts the fried fish and vegetables.

history.summary: Riesling was a pioneering experiment for the Vaira family, planted in the high, cool hills of Barolo and bottled as the Petracine since the late 1990s.

milestones.event: Vajra bottles one of Piedmont's first serious dry Rieslings

wines.description: G.D. Vajra's pioneering Langhe Riesling from high, cool Barolo hills. Dry, mineral and built on racy acidity.

verified.address_quoted: Via delle Viole, 25 - Fraz. Vergne 12060 Barolo (CN)

wines.name: G.D. Vajra Langhe Nebbiolo

taste.summary: Vajra's Langhe Nebbiolo is a fresh, fragrant and early-drinking introduction to the grape, drawn from younger vines around Vergne.

pairings.why: Round tannin and red fruit carry the meat sauce.
Medium body suits poultry.
Bright acidity refreshes against cured meats.

history.summary: Vajra's Langhe Nebbiolo offers the grape's perfume in an accessible, earlier-drinking style.

milestones.event: Vajra bottles a fragrant, early-drinking Langhe Nebbiolo

wines.description: G.D. Vajra's fresh, fragrant Langhe Nebbiolo from young Vergne vines. An accessible introduction to the grape.

verified.address_quoted: Via delle Viole, 25 - Fraz. Vergne 12060 Barolo (CN)

wines.name: G.D. Vajra Dolcetto d'Alba

taste.summary: A benchmark Vajra Dolcetto, juicy and purple-fruited with the soft tannin and easy drinkability that define the everyday red of the Langhe.

pairings.why: Juicy dark fruit and low tannin suit tomato and cheese.
Soft, fruity body refreshes against cured meats.
Easygoing Dolcetto frames a simple egg dish.

history.summary: Dolcetto has been a staple of the Vaira family estate since its 1972 founding, made as a juicy, fragrant everyday red.

milestones.event: Dolcetto bottled from the estate's first vintages

wines.description: G.D. Vajra's benchmark Dolcetto d'Alba. Juicy purple fruit and soft tannin, the everyday red of the Langhe.

verified.address_quoted: Via delle Viole, 25 - Fraz. Vergne 12060 Barolo (CN)

wines.name: G.D. Vajra Langhe Freisa Kye

taste.summary: Kye is Vajra's serious, oak-aged take on Freisa, a near-forgotten Nebbiolo relative, giving a tannic, high-toned red with wild-berry character.

pairings.why: Freisa's structure and wild-berry edge meet gamey ragu.
The wine's firm tannin frames slow-cooked rabbit.
Alpine cheese softens the tannin.

history.summary: Freisa, a Piedmontese grape related to Nebbiolo, was championed by the Vaira family with the structured, age-worthy Kye bottling.

milestones.event: Vajra launches Kye as a serious dry Freisa

wines.description: G.D. Vajra's serious, oak-aged Freisa, a near-forgotten Nebbiolo relative. Tannic, high-toned and wild-berried.

verified.address_quoted: Via delle Viole, 25 - Fraz. Vergne 12060 Barolo (CN)

wines.name: Giacomo Conterno Barolo Riserva Monfortino

taste.summary: The summit of traditional Barolo, made only in great vintages from the Cascina Francia and Arione vineyards in Serralunga, with wild-swing fermentation and six to eight years in large old barrel before release.

pairings.why: The braise's depth meets Monfortino's vast structure and savoury core.
Serralunga power stands up to the platter of boiled meats.
Salt sharpens the wine's dried-cherry and tar register.

history.summary: Monfortino is Giacomo Conterno's reserve Barolo, historically the house's longest-aged wine and now sourced from the Cascina Francia (acquired 1974) and Arione (acquired 2015) vineyards in Serralunga d'Alba.

milestones.event: Giacomo Conterno establishes Monfortino as a long-aged reserve Barolo
Cascina Francia vineyard in Serralunga acquired
Arione vineyard added

wines.description: The summit of traditional Barolo, Giacomo Conterno's Monfortino Riserva from Serralunga's Cascina Francia and Arione. Made only in great years.

wines.name: Giacomo Conterno Barolo Cascina Francia

taste.summary: The benchmark traditional Serralunga Barolo, from the estate's own Cascina Francia vineyard, against which many producers measure their work: structured, savoury and built for decades.

pairings.why: Long-braised collagen melts against the firm Serralunga tannin.
Truffle resonates with the wine's savoury depth.
Hard alpine cheese mirrors the mineral spine.

history.summary: Cascina Francia is Giacomo Conterno's estate Barolo from the Serralunga vineyard purchased in 1974, first bottled at the end of the 1970s and regarded as a benchmark of classic Barolo.

milestones.event: First Cascina Francia Barolo from the estate's Serralunga vineyard

wines.description: Giacomo Conterno's benchmark Serralunga Barolo from the estate's own Cascina Francia vineyard. Structured, savoury and built for decades.

wines.name: Giacomo Conterno Barbera d'Alba Cascina Francia

taste.summary: A serious, age-worthy Barbera from the same Cascina Francia vineyard in Serralunga, given long ageing in large old barrel in the traditionalist Conterno manner.

pairings.why: Barbera's bright acidity cuts the rich meat filling.
The wine's fruit and acidity carry the meat sauce.
Cured pork meets the refreshing acid line.

history.summary: Conterno's Barbera d'Alba comes from the Cascina Francia estate in Serralunga, made in a structured, long-aged style unusual for the grape.

milestones.event: Cascina Francia vineyard acquired, source of the estate Barbera

wines.description: Giacomo Conterno's serious, long-aged Barbera d'Alba from the Cascina Francia estate in Serralunga. Structured and age-worthy.

wines.name: Bartolo Mascarello Barolo

taste.summary: A single blended Barolo co-fermented from four traditional crus (Cannubi, San Lorenzo and Rue in Barolo, Rocche in La Morra), expressing the house belief that the most complete Barolo comes from mutually compensating sites.

pairings.why: The braise meets the blend's structure and savoury depth.
Truffle resonates with the wine's tar and licorice register.
Salt sharpens the strawberry and dried-flower lift.

history.summary: Maria Teresa Mascarello continues her father Bartolo's philosophy of making a single blended Barolo, co-fermenting fruit from the family's holdings in Cannubi, San Lorenzo, Rue and Rocche rather than bottling single crus.

milestones.event: Mascarello family begins bottling Barolo in the village
Maria Teresa Mascarello takes over the cellar

wines.description: Bartolo Mascarello's single blended Barolo, co-fermented from four crus. A benchmark of soulful, traditional Nebbiolo.

wines.name: Bartolo Mascarello Barbera d'Alba

taste.summary: A traditional, energetic Barbera from the same Barolo-village family known for understatement and balance rather than oak or extraction.

pairings.why: Barbera's acidity lifts the cold veal and tuna.
The wine's bright fruit carries the meat sauce.
Acidity refreshes against cured fats.

history.summary: The Mascarello family has long bottled a balanced, traditional Barbera alongside its single blended Barolo.

milestones.event: Barbera bottled from the family's earliest vintages

wines.description: Bartolo Mascarello's traditional, energetic Barbera d'Alba. Understated and balanced, in the house's purist style.

wines.name: Giuseppe Mascarello Barolo Monprivato

taste.summary: The Monprivato cru in Castiglione Falletto, a near-monopole of Giuseppe Mascarello, gives a benchmark traditional Barolo of perfume, finesse and remarkable longevity.

pairings.why: The braise meets the cru's structure and red-fruit perfume.
Delicate game suits Monprivato's finesse.
Salt sharpens the wine's red-cherry lift.

history.summary: Monprivato in Castiglione Falletto is the historic cru of Giuseppe Mascarello, vinified as a single vineyard since the early 1970s and a reference point for classic Barolo.

milestones.event: Monprivato established as a single-vineyard Barolo

wines.description: Giuseppe Mascarello's benchmark Barolo from the Monprivato cru in Castiglione Falletto. Perfumed, fine and long-lived.

wines.name: Giuseppe Mascarello Barolo Villero

taste.summary: From the Villero cru in Castiglione Falletto, a structured, savoury traditional Barolo from one of the zone's great central hills.

pairings.why: Rich beef matches the cru's firm structure.
Truffle resonates with the savoury depth.
Hard cheese mirrors the mineral grip.

history.summary: Villero is a great central Castiglione Falletto cru from which Giuseppe Mascarello has long made a structured traditional Barolo.

milestones.event: Villero bottled as a single-vineyard Barolo

wines.description: Giuseppe Mascarello's structured Barolo from the Villero cru in Castiglione Falletto. Savoury and traditional.

wines.name: Poderi Aldo Conterno Barolo Granbussia Riserva

taste.summary: Aldo Conterno's flagship Riserva, a blend of the estate's oldest Bussia vines (Romirasco, Cicala and Colonnello) made only in superior years and cellar-aged at least ten years before release.

pairings.why: The braise meets the Riserva's vast structure.
Monforte power stands up to the boiled-meat platter.
Salt sharpens the wine's savoury depth.

history.summary: Granbussia is Aldo Conterno's reserve Barolo, selected from the oldest vines of the Romirasco, Cicala and Colonnello crus in Bussia and released only in the finest vintages.

milestones.event: Aldo Conterno establishes the Granbussia Riserva

wines.description: Aldo Conterno's flagship Barolo Riserva, blended from the oldest Bussia vines. Made only in superior years, aged a decade before release.

wines.name: Poderi Aldo Conterno Barolo Romirasco

taste.summary: The most powerful of Aldo Conterno's single crus, Romirasco sits at the heart of the Bussia amphitheatre and gives a deep, structured traditional Barolo.

pairings.why: Rich beef matches the cru's deep structure.
Truffle resonates with the savoury core.
Hard cheese mirrors the mineral grip.

history.summary: Romirasco is the highest and most structured of Aldo Conterno's Bussia crus, vinified as a single vineyard since the early 1980s.

milestones.event: Romirasco bottled as a single-vineyard Barolo

wines.description: Aldo Conterno's most powerful single cru, from the heart of the Bussia amphitheatre. Deep, structured traditional Barolo.

wines.name: Poderi Aldo Conterno Barolo Cicala

taste.summary: Cicala is the most aromatic and supple of Aldo Conterno's Bussia crus, giving a perfumed, fine-grained traditional Barolo.

pairings.why: Delicate game suits the cru's perfume.
Fine tannin frames the meat-stuffed pasta.
Salt sharpens the red-fruit lift.

history.summary: Cicala is one of the three Bussia crus Aldo Conterno bottles individually, prized for its aromatic, supple character.

milestones.event: Cicala bottled as a single-vineyard Barolo

wines.description: Aldo Conterno's most aromatic Bussia cru. Perfumed, supple and fine-grained traditional Barolo.

wines.name: Poderi Aldo Conterno Barolo Colonnello

taste.summary: Colonnello is the most elegant and balanced of Aldo Conterno's Bussia crus, a refined traditional Barolo of finesse.

pairings.why: Refined tannin frames the delicate meat.
The wine's elegance carries the egg-rich pasta.
Mild cheese frames the red fruit.

history.summary: Colonnello is the third of Aldo Conterno's individually bottled Bussia crus, regarded as the most elegant of the trio.
